translation_disclaimer: This English translation is provided for convenience and is pending professional legal review. It is not legally binding. Only the German version is the authoritative legal text. In case of any conflict, the German version prevails.

impressum.meta_description: Legal notice of GastroHub, provider of restaurant ordering systems without commissions.
impressum.title: Legal Notice
impressum.disclaimer_inline: The German version of this Legal Notice is the legally binding version. This English version is provided for informational purposes only.
impressum.diensteanbieter_h: Information pursuant to §5 DDG (German Digital Services Act)
impressum.diensteanbieter_note: The address listed under c/o MDC is the address authorized for service of process. MDC is authorized to accept postal correspondence and judicial service on behalf of the service provider.
impressum.ustg_body: Pursuant to §19 UStG (small-business rule of the German VAT Act), no VAT is charged or shown. A VAT identification number is therefore not held.
impressum.mstv_h: Responsible person for content pursuant to §18 para. 2 MStV (German Interstate Media Treaty)
impressum.haftung_content_body: The contents of this website were created with the greatest possible care. No warranty is given for the accuracy, completeness or timeliness of the content. As a service provider, we are responsible for our own content on these pages under §7 para. 1 DDG and general law. Under §§8 to 10 DDG, as a service provider we are not obliged to monitor transmitted or stored third-party information or to investigate circumstances suggesting unlawful activity. Obligations to remove or block the use of information under general law remain unaffected. Such liability is only possible from the point in time at which knowledge of a specific legal infringement becomes known. Upon becoming aware of corresponding legal infringements, we will remove these contents immediately.
impressum.haftung_links_body: Our offer contains links to external third-party websites over whose contents we have no influence. Therefore, we cannot assume any liability for these external contents. The respective provider or operator of the linked pages is always responsible for their content. The linked pages were checked for possible legal violations at the time of linking. Illegal content was not recognizable at the time of linking. A permanent content control of the linked pages is not reasonable without concrete indications of a legal violation. Upon becoming aware of legal violations, we will remove such links immediately.
impressum.urheberrecht_body: The content and works on these pages created by the service provider are subject to German copyright law. Reproduction, processing, distribution and any kind of exploitation outside the limits of copyright law require the written consent of the respective author. Downloads and copies of this site are only permitted for private, non-commercial use. Insofar as the content on this site was not created by the service provider, the copyrights of third parties are respected. In particular, third-party content is marked as such. Should you nevertheless become aware of a copyright infringement, please inform us accordingly. Upon becoming aware of legal violations, we will remove such content immediately.
impressum.odr_intro: European Commission platform for online dispute resolution (ODR):
impressum.odr_outro: Our email address can be found above in the Legal Notice.
impressum.vsbg_body: We are neither willing nor obliged to participate in dispute resolution proceedings before a consumer arbitration board (§36 VSBG).

cookie_richtlinie.meta_description: Cookie policy of GastroHub. We use only technically necessary cookies. No analytics, no tracking, no advertising.
cookie_richtlinie.title: Cookie Policy
cookie_richtlinie.s1_body: Cookies are small text files placed in your browser when you visit a website. They enable technical functionality (for example, remembering a choice you've made). Local Storage and Session Storage in your browser perform similar functions and are also referred to as "cookies" below.
cookie_richtlinie.s2_intro: On gastrohub.dev we use only technically necessary cookies. Currently these are:
cookie_richtlinie.s2_consent_body: Stores your decision on the cookie banner ("accepted" or "declined") so the notice is not shown again. No personal data is stored. Storage duration: until you delete it yourself.
cookie_richtlinie.s2_no_analytics: We use no analytics cookies, no tracking cookies, no marketing or advertising cookies, and no third-party cookies.
cookie_richtlinie.s3_body1: The legal basis for using technically necessary cookies is § 25 para. 2 no. 2 TTDSG (German Telecommunications Telemedia Data Protection Act). Under that provision, consent is not required where storage of information, or access to information already stored, is strictly necessary so that the provider of a telemedia service can deliver a service the user has expressly requested.
cookie_richtlinie.s3_body2: Because we use no cookies that would require consent, our cookie banner serves only to inform you and to obtain a voluntary acknowledgement of this notice.
cookie_richtlinie.s4_body1: You can view, individually or fully delete cookies and Local Storage at any time in your browser settings, and block the acceptance of new cookies. How this works depends on your browser. You will find instructions in the help section of your respective browser (for example Chrome, Firefox, Safari, Edge).
cookie_richtlinie.s4_body2: If you delete the consent cookie, the cookie notice will be shown again on your next visit.
cookie_richtlinie.s5_body_prefix: You will find further information on the processing of your personal data in our

datenschutz.meta_description: Privacy policy of GastroHub. GDPR-compliant, European servers, Stripe Connect as payment processor.
datenschutz.title: Privacy Policy
datenschutz.disclaimer_inline: The German version of this Privacy Policy is the legally binding version. This English version is provided for informational purposes only.
datenschutz.s1_body1: The following notes give an overview of what happens to your personal data when you visit this website or use our restaurant ordering platform. Personal data is any data with which you can be personally identified.
datenschutz.s1_body2: This policy covers both the website at gastrohub.dev and the ordering platform operated by GastroHub that restaurant operators use on their end.
datenschutz.s2_intro: The data controller for the purposes of the GDPR is:
datenschutz.s3_marketing_body: The marketing website is hosted via GitHub Pages (GitHub, Inc., 88 Colin P Kelly Jr St, San Francisco, CA 94107, USA). When the website is accessed, your browser automatically transmits technical data (for example IP address, browser type, time of access) to GitHub's servers. The legal basis is Art. 6 para. 1 lit. f GDPR (legitimate interest in technically faultless provision). Transfer to the USA takes place on the basis of the EU-US Data Privacy Framework. GitHub is DPF-certified.
datenschutz.s3_platform_body: Ordering-platform application data is processed exclusively on servers in Germany (Frankfurt) by Hetzner Online GmbH, Industriestr. 25, 91710 Gunzenhausen, Germany. Purpose: application hosting and data storage. DPA concluded under Art. 28 GDPR. No transfer to third countries.
datenschutz.s4_body1: If you contact us by email or via the mailto link offered on the website, we process your details (name, email address, content of the message) exclusively to handle your enquiry. The legal basis is Art. 6 para. 1 lit. b GDPR (pre-contractual measures) or Art. 6 para. 1 lit. f GDPR (legitimate interest in answering enquiries).
datenschutz.s4_body2: Contact enquiries are deleted at the latest after 12 months, unless statutory retention obligations require otherwise.
datenschutz.s5_intro: When you book our platform as a restaurant operator, we process your master data for contract performance and invoicing:
datenschutz.s5_outro: The legal basis is Art. 6 para. 1 lit. b GDPR (performance of contract). Retention duration follows the commercial and tax-law retention periods (generally 10 years under §147 AO of the German Fiscal Code).
datenschutz.s6_body1: End customers who order at a restaurant via the platform provided by GastroHub enter personal data (name, delivery address, email, phone number, order details). The primary controller for this data is the restaurant operator. GastroHub processes this data as a processor within the meaning of Art. 28 GDPR, exclusively on the instructions of and as directed by the restaurant operator.
datenschutz.s6_body2: A Data Processing Agreement (DPA) under Art. 28 GDPR is concluded with each restaurant operator, setting out the obligations and guarantees. The DPA is concluded before the first processing of end-customer personal data begins. The data is processed exclusively on European servers and is not passed on to third parties, except to the payment service providers named in section 7.
datenschutz.s7_body1: For the processing of payments on the restaurant platform we use Stripe Connect (Stripe Payments Europe, Limited, 1 Grand Canal Street Lower, Grand Canal Dock, Dublin, Ireland). Stripe is an independent controller within the meaning of the GDPR.
datenschutz.s7_body2_pre: With an order paid online, payment data (in particular card data) is transmitted directly to Stripe. We do not store or see full card data. Stripe processes the data for payment processing and fraud prevention. The terms can be found at
datenschutz.s7_body3: In the commission models, Stripe automatically retains the agreed platform fee per transaction and forwards it to GastroHub. Transaction metadata (order timestamp, amount, restaurant identifier) is transmitted to us, but no payment instruments and no full end-customer data. For the processing of this metadata for billing and accounting purposes, GastroHub is an independent controller. The legal basis is Art. 6 para. 1 lit. b GDPR and Art. 6 para. 1 lit. c GDPR (statutory retention obligations).
datenschutz.s7_body4: Stripe reserves the right to transfer data to affiliates outside the EU, safeguarded by Standard Contractual Clauses and the EU-US Data Privacy Framework. Stripe is DPF-certified.
datenschutz.s8_body1: We operate a public business profile on Google Business Profile (Google Ireland Limited, Gordon House, Barrow Street, Dublin 4, Ireland). When you visit our profile or interact with it (for example reviews, questions), Google processes personal data as an independent controller. We have no influence on the nature and scope of this processing. The legal basis for our operation of the profile is Art. 6 para. 1 lit. f GDPR (legitimate interest in external representation and customer communication).
datenschutz.s9_body: We currently do not maintain profiles on social media platforms. If this changes, we will update this privacy policy accordingly.
datenschutz.s10_body: The ordering platform constitutes an electronic commerce platform within the meaning of §312i BGB (German Civil Code). The requirements of the Button-Lösung (button solution) under §312j para. 3 BGB are implemented in the checkout flow.
datenschutz.s11_body1: This website uses only technically necessary cookies required for the operation of the website. No tracking, analytics or marketing cookies are used. As no consent-based cookies are deployed, no cookie banner is shown.
datenschutz.s11_body2: Legal basis for technically necessary cookies: §25 para. 2 no. 2 TTDSG.
datenschutz.s12_body: Each time the website is accessed, technical access data (server log files) is automatically recorded by the hosting provider. This includes IP address, date and time, the URL accessed, transferred data volume, referrer URL, user agent. This data is processed exclusively to ensure faultless operation and to fend off misuse, and is deleted within 90 days at the latest. The legal basis is Art. 6 para. 1 lit. f GDPR.
datenschutz.s13_body: We apply technical and organisational measures to protect your data against loss, manipulation and unauthorised access. The connection to the website is SSL/TLS-encrypted. Access to the platform is password-protected and logged.
datenschutz.s14_body: In the event of a personal data breach likely to result in a risk to the rights and freedoms of natural persons, we will notify the competent supervisory authority within 72 hours in accordance with Art. 33 GDPR. Where the breach is likely to result in a high risk to affected individuals, they will be informed without undue delay in accordance with Art. 34 GDPR.
datenschutz.s15_body: No automated decision-making, including profiling within the meaning of Art. 22 GDPR, takes place.
datenschutz.s16_intro: You have the following rights vis-à-vis us regarding your personal data:
datenschutz.s16_li4: Right to restriction of processing (Art. 18 GDPR)
datenschutz.s16_li6: Right to object to processing (Art. 21 GDPR)
datenschutz.s16_li7: Right to withdraw a given consent (Art. 7 para. 3 GDPR)
datenschutz.s16_outro_pre: To exercise your rights, please email
datenschutz.s17_h: Right to complain to the supervisory authority
datenschutz.s17_intro: You have the right to lodge a complaint with a data protection supervisory authority about the processing of your personal data. The competent supervisory authority for GastroHub is:
datenschutz.s17_authority_l1: State Commissioner for Data Protection and Freedom of Information Baden-Württemberg
datenschutz.s18_li3: Contract and master data of restaurant customers: 10 years (§147 AO)
datenschutz.s18_li5: End-customer data on the ordering platform: as instructed by the restaurant operator in the DPA
datenschutz.s18_li6: Cookies (technically necessary): until end of browser session
datenschutz.s19_body: We reserve the right to update this privacy policy if legal changes or adjustments to our services make this necessary. The respective current version is always available on this page.

agb.meta_description: Terms and conditions of GastroHub. Restaurant ordering platform with one-time payment or commission model.
agb.title: General Terms and Conditions (AGB)
agb.p1_body: These General Terms and Conditions (AGB) apply to all contracts between Osama Farroukh, c/o MDC, Welserstraße 3, 87463 Dietmannsried, Germany (hereinafter "GastroHub") and the restaurant operator (hereinafter "customer") concerning the provision of the GastroHub ordering platform and related services. Deviating terms of the customer shall not become part of the contract unless GastroHub expressly agrees to them in writing. The services are exclusively aimed at entrepreneurs within the meaning of § 14 BGB (German Civil Code).
agb.p2_body1: GastroHub provides the customer with a browser-based ordering platform, consisting of a public ordering page for end customers and an admin dashboard for managing menu, orders, delivery zones, opening hours and other operational functions. The exact scope of features is set out in the description on gastrohub.dev at the time the contract is concluded.
agb.p2_body2: Optionally, GastroHub sets up the platform for the customer, performs the initial menu maintenance from existing sources, and sets up a subdomain or links a domain provided by the customer. Unless expressly agreed otherwise, these services are included in the setup price.
agb.p2_body3: The contractual relationship between the restaurant operator and the end customer is established directly between them. GastroHub provides the platform within the meaning of § 312i BGB and implements the Button-Lösung (button solution) pursuant to § 312j para. 3 BGB. The restaurant operator is responsible for fulfilling the information and instruction obligations vis-à-vis end customers.
agb.p3_body: The contract is concluded by the customer's acceptance of an individual offer from GastroHub in text form (for example by email). The pricing models presented on gastrohub.dev do not constitute a binding offer but an invitation to submit an offer (invitatio ad offerendum). The contract is concluded exclusively by acceptance of an individual offer in text form.
agb.p4_intro: The customer chooses one of the following pricing models:
agb.p4_li1_body: Setup fee from €2,500 (fixed price in the offer) plus €149 per month for hosting, domain, email and basic maintenance. No commission per order applies. Technical support is billed separately on a time-and-materials basis.
agb.p4_li2_body: Setup fee from €499. GastroHub receives 5% per processed order, with a minimum of €149 per month. Maintenance and technical support are billed separately on a time-and-materials basis.
agb.p4_li3_body: Setup fee from €499. GastroHub receives 7% per processed order, with a minimum of €249 per month. Maintenance, updates and technical support are included in the price.
agb.p4_outro1: The exact setup fee depends on the scope of services agreed in the offer (in particular the number of menu items, delivery zones, languages and migration effort) and is stated bindingly before the contract is concluded.
agb.p4_outro2: In the commission model, the commission is automatically retained per transaction via Stripe Connect (Application Fee), shown to the customer and paid out to GastroHub. The monthly minimum is invoiced separately for the difference if the commission accrued in the month is below the minimum. Monthly flat fees (hosting and basic maintenance), one-time amounts and separately billed maintenance or support hours are due by invoice with a payment term of 14 days.
agb.p5_body: Pursuant to § 19 UStG (small-business rule of the German VAT Act), no VAT is charged or shown. All amounts in this agreement and on invoices are net amounts without separate VAT.
agb.p6_body1: The customer shall provide all content necessary for setup and operation (menu, images, logo, opening hours, delivery zones, legal information) in good time and ensure its legal admissibility. The customer is in particular responsible for the completeness and accuracy of allergen and additive labelling.
agb.p6_body2: The customer designates at least one person responsible for the platform and ensures confidential handling of access credentials.
agb.p7_body: For online payment processing, the customer must set up a Stripe Connect account. The contractual partner for payment processing is Stripe (Stripe Payments Europe, Limited, Dublin, Ireland). Stripe's terms apply additionally. GastroHub assumes no liability for availability or functioning of Stripe services.
agb.p8_body_pre: Insofar as GastroHub processes personal data of restaurant end customers on behalf of the customer, the parties conclude a separate Data Processing Agreement (DPA) under Art. 28 GDPR. The DPA is concluded before the first processing of personal data of the restaurant operator's end customers begins. Further information on data processing can be found in our
agb.p9_body: GastroHub endeavours to achieve the highest possible availability of the platform but does not warrant any specific availability. Maintenance windows are scheduled, where possible, outside typical ordering hours. Outages due to force majeure, failures of upstream suppliers (hosting, Stripe, DNS) or third-party attacks do not give rise to damage claims, insofar as GastroHub is not responsible for them.
agb.p10_body1: GastroHub is liable without limitation for damages based on intentional or grossly negligent breach of duty, and for damages arising from injury to life, body or health. In the case of slightly negligent breach of an essential contractual duty (cardinal duty, the fulfilment of which is essential to the proper performance of the contract and on whose observance the customer regularly relies), liability is limited to the foreseeable damage typical for this type of contract. In the case of slightly negligent breach of non-essential contractual duties, liability is excluded to the extent permitted by law. Liability for loss of profit, indirect damages and consequential damages is excluded to the extent permitted by law.
agb.p10_body2: For content the customer publishes via the platform (in particular menu, images, texts), the customer is solely responsible. The customer indemnifies GastroHub against third-party claims in connection with such content.
agb.p11_body1: The contract term is at least 12 months for all three pricing models (one-time payment, 5% commission and 7% all-inclusive), starting from the contract start date stated in the offer or, unless otherwise agreed, from the first provision of the ordering platform.
agb.p11_body2: After the minimum term expires, the contract automatically renews for a further 12 months at a time, unless terminated in text form with 30 days' notice to the end of the respective contract period.
agb.p11_body3: Upon the termination taking effect, the ordering platform is deactivated. Commissions already accrued, monthly flat fees, setup fees and support or maintenance hours already provided remain unaffected and fall due in full.
agb.p11_body4: The right to extraordinary termination for cause remains unaffected for both parties. Terminations require text form.
agb.p12_h: § 12 Choice of law and place of jurisdiction
agb.p12_body: The law of the Federal Republic of Germany applies, excluding the UN Convention on Contracts for the International Sale of Goods (CISG). The exclusive place of jurisdiction for all disputes arising from this contract is Stuttgart, Germany, where the customer is a merchant, a legal entity under public law or a special fund under public law. GastroHub is also entitled to sue at the customer's general place of jurisdiction.
agb.p13_body: Should individual provisions of these terms be or become invalid or unenforceable, the validity of the remaining provisions remains unaffected. In place of the invalid or unenforceable provision, the valid and enforceable provision shall apply that comes closest to the economic purpose of the invalid or unenforceable provision.
